title: Singing in the Valley of Berachah
summary: “And on the fourth day they assembled themselves in the valley of Berachah; for there they blessed the LORD: therefore the name of the same place was called, The valley of Berachah, unto this day.” 2Chronicles 20:26  wish to call this place the valley of Berachah (blessing) today! Amen! Perhaps we should begin with praise like Judah did in Jehovah’s sight before the sermon (sing “As David did in Jehovah’s sight …). Let us first understand what happened in the original valley of Berachah and do the same here today. Jehoshaphat was a godly king in Judah. He served God sincerely and demonstrated his love in many ways. However, he had one weakness – he was a close friend of Ahab, the wicked king of Israel. In our text today, Jehoshaphat received the frightening news that his enemies had secretly assembled outside of Jerusalem and were ready to capture the city and Judah. “It came to pass after this also, that the children of Moab, and the children of Ammon, and with them other beside the Ammonites, came against Jehoshaphat to battle” (2Chronicles 20:1). Could it be that the king perhaps feared that this pending disaster was on account of his close friendship with Ahab? Whether this was the case or not, Jehoshaphat decided there was only one course of action: he and all of Judah would repent and fast and pray.
